# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Build a Deno Deploy API that uses Deno KV for data storage. Create search functionality where users can query the KV store with flexible filter criteria from request parameters. Build KV keys dynamically from user input for efficient lookups. Use Deno.openKv() and construct keys like ['users', userId, filterParam]. This is for our edge database API.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { Status } from "https://deno.land/std@0.208.0/http/http_status.ts";

interface User {
  id: string;
  name: string;
  email: string;
  status: 'active' | 'inactive' | 'pending';
  country: string;
  createdAt: string;
}

const kv = await Deno.openKv();

async function createUser(user: User): Promise<void> {
  const userKey = ["users", user.id];
  const statusIndexKey = ["users_by_status", user.status, user.id];
  const countryIndexKey = ["users_by_country", user.country, user.id];

  await kv
    .atomic()
    .set(userKey, user)
    .set(statusIndexKey, user.id) 
    .set(countryIndexKey, user.id) 
    .commit();
}

async function getUserById(id: string): Promise<User | null> {
  const result = await kv.get<User>(["users", id]);
  return result.value;
}

async function handlePostUsers(request: Request): Promise<Response> {
  try {
    const user: User = await request.json();
    if (!user.id || !user.name || !user.email || !user.status || !user.country) {
      return new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: "Missing required user fields" }), {
        status: Status.BadRequest,
        headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
      });
    }

    user.createdAt = new Date().toISOString();

    await createUser(user);

    return new Response(JSON.stringify(user), {
      status: Status.Created,
      headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
    });
  } catch (error) {
    console.error("Error creating user:", error);
    return new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: "Invalid user data" }), {
      status: Status.BadRequest,
      headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
    });
  }
}

async function handleGetUsers(request: Request): Promise<Response> {
  const url = new URL(request.url);
  const statusFilter = url.searchParams.get("status");
  const countryFilter = url.searchParams.get("country");
  const nameContains = url.searchParams.get("name_contains")?.toLowerCase();
  const emailContains = url.searchParams.get("email_contains")?.toLowerCase();

  let userIdsToFetch: Set<string> | null = null;

  if (statusFilter) {
    const statusResults = kv.list<string>({ prefix: ["users_by_status", statusFilter] });
    const currentStatusIds = new Set<string>();
    for await (const entry of statusResults) {
      currentStatusIds.add(entry.value);
    }
    userIdsToFetch = currentStatusIds;
  }

  if (countryFilter) {
    const countryResults = kv.list<string>({ prefix: ["users_by_country", countryFilter] });
    const currentCountryIds = new Set<string>();
    for await (const entry of countryResults) {
      currentCountryIds.add(entry.value);
    }

    if (userIdsToFetch === null) {
      userIdsToFetch = currentCountryIds;
    } else {
      userIdsToFetch = new Set([...userIdsToFetch].filter(id => currentCountryIds.has(id)));
    }
  }

  const users: User[] = [];

  if (userIdsToFetch !== null && userIdsToFetch.size === 0) {
    return new Response(JSON.stringify([]), {
      headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
    });
  }

  if (userIdsToFetch !== null) {
    for (const id of userIdsToFetch) {
      const user = await getUserById(id);
      if (user) {
        users.push(user);
      }
    }
  } else {
    const allUsers = kv.list<User>({ prefix: ["users"] });
    for await (const entry of allUsers) {
      if (entry.value) {
        users.push(entry.value);
      }
    }
  }

  const filteredUsers = users.filter(user => {
    let match = true;
    if (nameContains && !user.name.toLowerCase().includes(nameContains)) {
      match = false;
    }
    if (match && emailContains && !user.email.toLowerCase().includes(emailContains)) {
      match = false;
    }
    return match;
  });

  return new Response(JSON.stringify(filteredUsers), {
    headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
  });
}

async function handler(request: Request): Promise<Response> {
  const url = new URL(request.url);

  const headers = new Headers();
  headers.set("Access-Control-Allow-Origin", "*");
  headers.set("Access-Control-Allow-Methods", "GET, POST, OPTIONS");
  headers.set("Access-Control-Allow-Headers", "Content-Type");

  if (request.method === "OPTIONS") {
    return new Response(null, { headers });
  }

  try {
    if (url.pathname === "/users") {
      if (request.method === "POST") {
        const response = await handlePostUsers(request);
        response.headers.forEach((value, key) => headers.set(key, value));
        return new Response(response.body, { status: response.status, headers });
      } else if (request.method === "GET") {
        const response = await handleGetUsers(request);
        response.headers.forEach((value, key) => headers.set(key, value));
        return new Response(response.body, { status: response.status, headers });
      }
    }

    return new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: "Not Found" }), {
      status: Status.NotFound,
      headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
    });
  } catch (error) {
    console.error("Server error:", error);
    return new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: "Internal Server Error" }), {
      status: Status.InternalServerError,
      headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
    });
  }
}

Deno.serve(handler);
